title: Paul Saladino MD: What I Eat In A Day (2024)

chunks.title: Introduction to Animal-Based Diet
chunks.summary: The presenter introduces their animal-based diet, mentioning the components such as meat, organs, fruit, honey, and raw dairy. They emphasize flexibility in adopting this diet by using it as a blueprint for individual needs.
chunks.transcript: what's up guys I'm going to show you what I eat in a day in October of 2024 I basically still eat an animal-based diet it's meat it's organs it's fruit it's honey it's raw dairy tweak some things since last year's video so I want to show you all the details of this give you some ideas of how to create this type of a diet in your life it's something that I think helps a lot of people I don't think you need to eat exactly like me I want this to be helpful as a blueprint and you can add or subtract from it based on what works for you in case you're not familiar with my stuff I'm a board certified medical doctor I spend most of my time in Costa Rica I make free educational content because I feel like the mainstream medical system is not

chunks.title: Morning Routine and Pre-Surf Meal
chunks.summary: The morning routine includes grounding, stretching, and consuming raw goat milk mixed with honey and creatine. After surfing, breakfast consists of scrambled eggs, avocado, mango, and watermelon juice, with a unique method of preparing the scrambled eggs to retain nutrients.
chunks.transcript: thing I do in the morning is go outside touch the Earth with my bare feet do some grounding do some stretching get the sunlight in my eyes and then basically within half an hour of waking up I'll have my first pre- surf mini Meal which is raw goats milk from a local Farm I've actually been to this Farm they're incredible people I've milked the goats there I've seen the goats they take really great care of their goats so I have raw goat milk from the farm I add some glyphosate free honey and I add about 5 grams of creatine I might have a little bit of watermelon juice before I go surf but that is pretty much it I'm in the car and I'm in the ocean I try to be in the ocean by 6:00 a.m. I'll surf for a couple hours and then I come back and eat breakfast after I get home from surfing hopefully I caught a few waves I'll make scrambled eggs avocado mango slices and some watermelon juice which I make myself but there's a secret to the scrambled eggs so I've been trying to perfect the scrambled eggs maybe you'll disagree with my methods what I do is this I separate the yolks and the whites I add salt to the whites before I scramble them I scramble just the white slowly taking them off the heat to scramble then putting them back I'm trying to get the whites as fluffy as possible and then I take the raw yolks and I know this is controversial I take the raw yolks and I add those to The Scrambled whites at the end because i' probably rather just not cook the yolks or undercook the yolks when I make hardboiled eggs I like the yolks runny I just like runny eggs so I thought why not just add the yolks at the end and not really overcook them so it looks different than regular scrambled eggs but it tastes amazing and the yolks are basically if you're worried about getting sick like salmona or campact from raw yolks you don't really need to be concerned about this if those are on the outside of the shell which is rare but possible if the yolk membrane is still intact it hasn't actually touched the outside of the shell and the chance of contamination is very very low if you're really worried about that stuff fine cook your yolks too but I feel fine just cooking the whites and eating the yolks raw which I kind of mix in there or I just heat them a very small amount so I'm trying to get the fluffiest scrambled eggs with the most amount of nutrients and the Y yolk that haven't been heated that's just how I do it you guys decide if you like it that way but I try to make the scrambled eggs as good as possible the other thing to think about when you're doing eggs is that if you want to just eat the yolk raw you can but don't eat the whites raw egg whites contain a compound called avidin which binds Biotin and biotin is something you need for Hair Skin and Nails just overall health you don't want to be binding up the biotin that's found in the egg yolk by eating the whites raw so don't eat raw egg whites I know bodybuilders did it for years and years and years but I think there's a better way sometimes people think avocado is a vegetable wrong avocado is a fruit why do I make the distinction vegetables are the leaves the stems the roots and the seeds of plants they contain more defense chemicals I'm not a huge fan of them if you can tolerate vegetables that's great but for people like me and a lot of people with autoimmune disease or gut issues cutting out or eliminating vegetables seems to helps with those conditions fruit we know has much lower amounts of plant- Defense chemicals this is the whole idea of an animal-based diet or a carnivore diet you guys know that I used to do a carnivore diet have now transition to this type of eating why do I call it an animal-based diet because it is based around animal Foods what have I had so far today I've had animal milk I've had goat milk I've had some eggs and then I've added in some of the least toxic plant Foods you'll see as I go through my day that I'm eating fruit and I'm eating meat and I'm eating animal foods and that's basically how I compose my diet the majority of my calories are coming from animal Foods things like eggs things like beef Tallow things like steak or meat things like liver heart and things like milk or yogurt and this kind of stuff and then I'm adding in the least toxic plant foods for most people again if you're thriving don't change anything about your diet a lot of people can tolerate vegetables especially if they're fermented or well cooked but for those of us with gut issues or autoimmune issues that seem to be recalcitrant cutting those things out is a powerful tool and a lot of doctors don't talk about this at all so that's my breakfast

chunks.title: Understanding Animal-Based vs. Carnivore Diets
chunks.summary: The presenter clarifies their transition from a strict carnivore diet to an animal-based diet, highlighting the benefits of focusing on animal foods while incorporating the least toxic plant foods. They discuss the importance of food quality in addressing health issues like autoimmune diseases.
chunks.transcript: diet or a carnivore diet you guys know that I used to do a carnivore diet have now transition to this type of eating why do I call it an animal-based diet because it is based around animal Foods what have I had so far today I've had animal milk I've had goat milk I've had some eggs and then I've added in some of the least toxic plant Foods you'll see as I go through my day that I'm eating fruit and I'm eating meat and I'm eating animal foods and that's basically how I compose my diet the majority of my calories are coming from animal Foods things like eggs things like beef Tallow things like steak or meat things like liver heart and things like milk or yogurt and this kind of stuff and then I'm adding in the least toxic plant

chunks.title: Lunch Components and Health Considerations
chunks.summary: Lunch features grass-fed skirt steak, cheese, and fresh-squeezed orange juice, along with considerations about salt quality due to potential heavy metal contamination. The presenter shares their perspective on fruits and juices, arguing against common misconceptions about their health impacts.
chunks.transcript: lunch usually looks like between breakfast and lunch I usually do research podcasting thinking about social media content creative stuff eat around noon or 1 p.m. during the day and I'm usually going to eat something like skirt steak it's grass-fed grass finish regeneratively raised and I think about the salt that I put on the steak so I used to not be so keyed into this but I saw a recent analysis of many sea salts that a lot of us use and I actually really like the Jacobson one I don't have any affiliation with that company but if you look at the heavy metals and the Jacobson sea salt they're pretty low in contrast to this I used to use this sea salt but I don't anymore this actually has 553 parts per billion of lead which is a significant amount if you do the math 8 to n grams of that sea salt the Celtic Sea salt will put you over the limit in California for your daily amount of lead so know what the heavy metals are in your salt again I have no affiliation with the Jacobson brand I just was looking for a good brand of sea salt with very low levels of heavy metals I'm also thinking about microplastics but I was actually surprised and I'll do a separate video on this on the amount of metals in a lot of the sea salts we use um another good one is the diamond kosher salt which is not a sea salt but that one had essentially no heavy metals again no affiliation with any of those salt Brands so grass-fed grass finished skirt steak regeneratively raised local butcher got some parmesano regano cheese about 100 grams I am going to do some fresh squeezed orange juice I get all of my produce from farmers markets here it's all organic or so they tell me and I like to make my own Orange juice I don't worry about the sugar and orange juice I'm very active and there's tons of research showing that orange juice watermelon juice grape juice all the juices really improve endothelial function inside of your blood vessels they decrease lipid peroxidation they decrease DNA damage so I'm getting fiber from if I'm eating watermelon without the juice I'm getting fiber from cucumber I'm getting fiber from fruit that I'm eating like this Rotan or mamino I don't worry about trying to do massive amounts of fiber in the day I also don't necessarily fear it anymore I eat a mix of fruit juice and fruit throughout the day I get my carbohydrates also from honey as you've seen and so I don't worry about orange juice obviously scale the amount of carbohydrates in your diet to how active you are but I do not believe and I will make this very clear I do not believe there is any evidence that fruit or fruit juice contribute to insulin resistance or diabetes in any way shape or form in humans in fact there is good evidence that watermelon juice and many of the fruit juices actually improve insulin sensitivity and improve gluc glucose tolerance there's actually been a study done where they gave people watermelon juice interestingly enough when they were doing a glucose tolerance test and those patients glucose tolerance actually improved when they had the watermelon juice so don't fear fruit and fruit juice if you find other sources of carbohydrates work better for you that's great this is what works for me I've got some cucumber here cucumber is a fruit not a vegetable again I'm not a huge fan of the leaves the stems The Roots the seeds of plants I feel better without it my autoimmune issues which are eczema and Asthma are much better when I don't eat vegetables but if you can eat vegetables knock yourself out um so I got cucumber mamino we call them in Costa Rica they're also called rambutan skirt steak parmesano regano cheese and orange juice that I squeeze myself with some very low heavy metal salt that's my

chunks.title: Dinner and Raw Liver Consumption
chunks.summary: Dinner includes grass-fed ground beef, raw liver, dragon fruit, and more watermelon juice. The presenter explains their method of consuming raw liver and discusses the nutritional benefits of organs, emphasizing the ease of incorporating them into the diet.
chunks.transcript: guys then okay dinner time it started to rain in Costa Rica which happens this time of the year I've got some grass-fed ground beef ground beef is amazing it has a lot of collagenous parts of the animal in it a lot of the stuff that's a little tougher and it's super affordable so I like ground beef for collagen and I like it because it's affordable again grass-fed grass finished ground beef that I actually got at a farmers market here I got some raw liver um and and I just do it raw I freeze it before I trust the Butcher and I trust that the liver is high quality there's always a risk to eating anything raw whether it's raw milk or raw organs but I've never gotten sick from eating raw liver knock on wood I've got some dragon fruit that stuff is amazing it's much more available here in the tropics and it's delicious and I've got some more watermelon juice and raw milk with honey if you can't eat liver if you don't have access to liver or you don't want to eat liver which you could also cook by the way something like Harden soil supplements might be a great option for you desiccated freeze-dried organs are pretty darn good if you're not going to do the real thing but getting organs in your life is a huge upgrade to your nutrition if you look at things like chronometer and I'm going to do a chronometer video followup to this whole what I eaten a day where I show you guys the breakdown I'll show you the micronutrients the macros and even having a half an ounce of liver in your diet every day is going to improve things like copper malinam biotin vitamin A it's a huge upgrade to your life having even a little bit of organs a lot of days I'll add some heart as well but if you don't want to do that do freeze-dried organs like hardened soil okay so let me show you guys how I do liver I just take the raw piece in the mouth swallow it Down the Hatch just like that you don't have to do that I just feel like that's the easiest way to do it you could also cook it if you like the taste you can chew it so that's my

chunks.title: Daily Nutritional Insights and Conclusion
chunks.summary: In the conclusion, the presenter summarizes the day's meals while reiterating the significance of an animal-based diet. They encourage viewers to focus on nutrient-dense foods that support overall health while correcting misconceptions about carbohydrates and grains.
chunks.transcript: animal- based diet it's organs like liver heart meat grass-fed beef and skirt steak at lunch fruit I had dragon fruit I had mango I had mamino I had watermelon juice I had orange juice today and raw dairy I had raw goats milk with honey and I had raw parmesano regano cheese which is a cow cheese so this type of a diet has helped me feel really good if you guys know my story a carnivore diet helped me understand that a lot of foods that we think of as healthy were actually triggering my Eczema but long-term ketosis did not work well for me I don't think it works well for a lot of people again I don't fear fruit I don't fear fruit juice I don't think these things cause diabetes they don't cause insulin resistance scale them to your activity level but there's really no evidence that these things are bad for us uh at all so again use carbohydrates that that work for you some people find sweet potatoes work better or white potatoes just be aware that white potatoes are a night shade they do contain some oxalates just like sweet potatoes so scale that appropriately I'm not a huge fan of grains I've done previous videos about why I don't love oatmeal I'm not a fan of wheat but if you can tolerate sourdough more power to you I'm kind of jealous but this is just what makes me feel better the whole idea here is just thinking about what are the most nutrient rich foods that you can eat that help you feel the best that help you improve your gut issues or your autoimmune issues that give you good libido good sleep good muscle tone that help you lose weight and this is an option that I think works for a lot of people animal based diet hopefully this video helps you guys understand how I construct an animal based diet every day
